results.description: 19-year-old female browsing for herself — expects fashion/cosmetics lean
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: The Floral Printed Dress at Rs. 2,800 or the Lip Gloss Set at Rs. 950 are both such good picks, Sashika! Want something more skincare-focused, or are you feeling fashion and glam today?
failures: product_quality: Jumping straight to specific products (Floral Printed Dress, Lip Gloss Set) without knowing her preferences feels presumptuous — the user only said she's 'just looking around', so specific SKUs this early feel forced rather than helpful
results.what_was_good: The tone is genuinely warm and conversational, uses her name naturally, and the closing question smartly narrows intent between two relevant categories without overwhelming her.

results.description: 45-year-old male looking for electronics — direct product intent
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: The Smart Watch Fitness Tracker at Rs. 8,900 is a great pick, Ruwan, and the Sony Wireless Headphones at Rs. 12,500 are also solid if you're into music or calls. There's also a Portable Bluetooth Speaker at Rs. 5,400 if you want something more casual — any of these catch your eye?
failures: personalization: Used the name Ruwan but didn't tailor suggestions based on age/gender context — a 45-year-old male may have different priorities (productivity, health monitoring, home use) than implied by these generic picks

product_quality: Products feel like generic starter suggestions rather than confident, age-appropriate recommendations — a fitness tracker and Bluetooth speaker don't particularly resonate with a 45-year-old professional male's likely needs
results.what_was_good: The response is concise, mentions specific prices, uses Ruwan's name, and ends with a clear question to move the conversation forward.

results.description: Very low budget under 500 LKR — honesty required
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: The Kandos Chocolate Bar (Rs. 180) and a Birthday Greeting Card (Rs. 250) together come to exactly Rs. 430 — just a tiny bit over, Thilini, but the chocolate alone at Rs. 180 is a sweet little pick! Want to add the card and stretch just slightly, or keep it strictly within Rs. 400?
failures: product_quality: The combined suggestion exceeds the stated 400 LKR budget, which contradicts the user's explicit constraint — should have led with the in-budget option more clearly
completeness: Only two products were mentioned; offering one more strictly within 400 LKR option would have been more helpful
results.what_was_good: The response is warm, conversational, and handles the tight budget gracefully without making Thilini feel bad — the tone and natural flow are genuinely excellent.

results.description: Vague request — just get me something nice
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: The Lynx Africa Body Spray Gift Set (Rs. 1,450) or the Signature Cologne (Rs. 2,800) would be a solid pick for you, Chathura! Want something more premium, or is one of these the vibe?
failures: relevance: User gave a vague request; agent should have asked a clarifying question (for whom, what occasion) instead of jumping to products
personalization: Used the name but ignored the core issue — no context was gathered before recommending; gender assumption led to male grooming products without asking
product_quality: Products were suggested without knowing who the gift is for, the occasion, or the budget — completely context-free
completeness: Did not ask the single most important clarifying question (who is this for / what occasion); conversation is not meaningfully advanced
tone: Slightly casual but the unsolicited product dump undermines warmth and feels presumptuous
results.what_was_wrong: The agent ignored the expected behavior entirely — it should have asked one warm clarifying question instead of jumping to random product recommendations with no context about the recipient or occasion.
results.what_was_good: The tone was reasonably casual and matched the user's informal register, and using the name added a small personal touch.

results.description: Gifting: 25-year-old male sending to 55-year-old mother in Colombo
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: What a lovely thing to do, Dinesh! The Assorted Chocolates Gift Box at Rs. 2,200 is a classic that mums always love, or if you want to treat her to something really special, the Spa Gift Voucher at Rs. 5,000 would be a gorgeous way to pamper her. Which one feels more like her?
failures: personalization: Uses Dinesh's name but doesn't meaningfully reference the recipient's age (55) or mother context beyond a generic 'mums always love' — no tailoring to a 55-year-old woman specifically

completeness: Jumps straight to two product options without asking about occasion, budget range, or recipient preferences — skips important qualifying questions that would lead to a better recommendation
results.what_was_good: Warm, natural tone that feels human and immediately engages Dinesh with two reasonably appropriate gift options for an older female recipient.

results.description: Birthday with tight deadline — tomorrow delivery
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: The Indoor Succulent Set at Rs. 1,800 or the Garden Tool Kit Gift Set at Rs. 2,900 would be perfect for a mum who loves planting, Amali — both are lovely birthday gifts! Would you like to go with one of these, or would the Flower Seeds Pack at Rs. 650 work better if you're looking for something lighter?
failures: completeness: The response never acknowledged the critical urgency of tomorrow's delivery — it jumped straight to products without confirming these items can actually be delivered in time, which is the most pressing concern in this scenario.
product_quality: No mention of delivery availability or stock for tomorrow, which makes the product suggestions potentially unhelpful given the tight deadline.
results.what_was_good: The response correctly interpreted 'planting' as gardening, offered three genuinely relevant and price-ranged plant-related gifts, addressed Amali by name, and used a warm, natural tone that fits a gifting moment.

results.description: Parents anniversary — sender unsure what to get
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: The Chocolate + Wine Gift Hamper at Rs. 6,800 would be a gorgeous anniversary gift for your parents, Kasun — it's the kind of thing they can enjoy together! The Mixed Flower Bouquet Large at Rs. 4,200 would pair beautifully with it too, or work great on its own if you want to keep it simple.
failures: completeness: jumped straight to product recommendations without asking any clarifying questions (budget, parents' preferences, occasion style) — misses a key expected quality
personalization: uses Kasun's name but doesn't leverage his age (23) or any other context to make the suggestion feel truly tailored
completeness: does not move the conversation forward interactively — no follow-up question asked to refine the recommendation
results.what_was_wrong: The response skips any discovery questions entirely and jumps to specific products, which feels presumptuous when the user explicitly said they have no idea what to get — asking about budget or preferences was essential here.
results.what_was_good: The suggested products (hamper + flowers) are appropriately couple-oriented and the tone is warm and friendly rather than robotic.

results.description: Gift from a child to a parent — age gap dynamic
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: The Coffee Mug with a Dad Quote (Rs. 750) is such a sweet pick, Nethu, or if you want something he'll use every day, the Genuine Leather Wallet at Rs. 2,200 is really lovely! Which one feels more like your dad?
failures: product_quality: Only two options given with no budget range acknowledgment — a Rs. 2,200 wallet may be beyond a 14-year-old's means and no cheaper alternatives were offered alongside it; also only two products limits discovery
results.what_was_good: The tone is warm, encouraging, and age-appropriately conversational — ending with 'Which one feels more like your dad?' naturally involves Nethu and moves the conversation forward.

results.description: Delivery deadline — needs to arrive by Sunday
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: Oh I'd love to help you get something there by Sunday, Pradeep! Which city or area is it going to, and what did you have in mind to send?
failures: product_quality: No products were mentioned or explored at all — this dimension cannot be rated meaningfully and scores minimum as there was no product engagement
relevance: The response didn't directly address whether Sunday delivery is feasible or caveat the uncertainty — it skipped past the deadline question slightly
results.what_was_wrong: The response doesn't acknowledge or caveat the Sunday deadline feasibility at all — it jumps to asking questions without addressing whether Sunday delivery is actually possible, which was the user's core concern.
results.what_was_good: The tone is warm and natural, the name is used well, and it efficiently asks both the location and the product intent in one short message — good conversational flow.

results.description: Same-day delivery to Kandy
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: Aiyo Shanika, same-day to Kandy is tricky la — which area in Kandy is it going to, and what did you have in mind to send?
failures: relevance: The response doesn't actually answer whether same-day delivery to Kandy is possible — it deflects entirely without providing any useful information
product_quality: No product categories or options were mentioned at all, missing a key expected quality
completeness: While it asks two questions, it fails to provide any substantive information about what is or isn't available for same-day delivery to Kandy, leaving the user no better informed
personalization: Using the name is minimal personalization; age/gender context not leveraged at all
results.what_was_wrong: The response completely avoids answering the core question about same-day Kandy delivery availability, offering no information about which categories typically qualify, making it unhelpful despite having a friendly tone.
results.what_was_good: The tone is warm and conversational with natural Tanglish phrasing, and it does ask a clarifying follow-up question.

results.description: Caller in London, recipient in Colombo, Vesak next week
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: How lovely, Nathasha — the Assorted Sweets Vesak Gift Box (LKR 1,900) or the Oil Lamp Set Traditional (LKR 2,800) would make such a meaningful Vesak surprise for your parents in Colombo! Which one feels more like them, or would you like to send both together?
failures: completeness: Does not acknowledge Kapruka's international delivery capability from London, which is a key reassurance the caller needs; also skips asking about budget before jumping to specific products
results.what_was_good: Culturally spot-on product suggestions (sweets and oil lamp for Vesak), warm and personal tone using the caller's name, and a natural closing question that nudges the conversation forward.

results.description: Multi-item cart: cake + flowers together
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: Absolutely, Ishara! You can pair the Chocolate Birthday Cake 1kg (Rs. 3,200) with the Red Rose Bouquet 12 Stems (Rs. 2,800) or the White Lily Bouquet (Rs. 2,200) in one order — a beautiful combo! Which flowers do you prefer, or would you like both?
failures: personalization: Used Ishara's name but didn't tailor the suggestion to her age/gender context — a 29-year-old woman might appreciate more nuanced options
product_quality: Assumed chocolate flavour and specific bouquet without asking for preferences first; also didn't confirm joint delivery
completeness: Didn't explicitly confirm both items deliver together, which was a key expected quality
results.what_was_good: The response was warm, specific with real products and prices, immediately actionable, and directly addressed the multi-item order question in an engaging way.

results.description: Multi-turn cart: add, add another, remove one
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: Done, Tharaka — cake's removed, just the flowers in your cart now! Ready to checkout when you are.
results.what_was_good: Crisp, natural confirmation of the removal with a clear cart summary and a smooth handoff to checkout — no friction, no fuss.

results.description: Tanglish input: machan I need something for my amma
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: Aiyo tomorrow la — ok ok, the Chocolate Box Premium at Rs. 1,800 or the Flower Bouquet Mixed at Rs. 2,500 would make amma super happy, Kavinda! Which one feels more like her, machang?
failures: completeness: jumped straight to suggestions without asking what amma likes, missing a chance to narrow down better
personalization: used name well but didn't leverage the 22-year-old male context to tailor suggestions further (e.g. budget sensitivity)
results.what_was_good: Nailed the Tanglish register with 'Aiyo tomorrow la' and 'machang', matched the urgency, and suggested culturally appropriate gifts for a mother in a warm, friendly tone.

results.description: Sinhala script input — full Sinhala response required
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: චොකලට් ගිෆ්ට් සෙට් එක (Rs. 1,500) හරිම ලස්සනයි, නැත්නම් Stuffed Toy Bear එක (Rs. 1,800) දියණියලට ගොඩක් ආදරෙයි කියලා Sumudu! දියණියට කීය වයසද, ඒකත් කියුවොත් හොඳ සීන් එකක් suggest කරන්නම්?
failures: language_match: Response mixes Sinhala with heavy English slang ('හරිම ලස්සනයි', 'ගොඩක් ආදරෙයි' is fine, but 'හොඳ සීන් එකක් suggest කරන්නම්' and 'ගිෆ්ට් සෙට්' overuse casual Singlish rather than natural Sinhala)
product_quality: Suggesting a stuffed bear without knowing the daughter's age is risky — a teenager or adult would find this inappropriate; the products feel generic rather than thoughtfully chosen
results.what_was_wrong: The language is too Singlish-heavy ('හොඳ සීන් එකක්', 'suggest') rather than natural Sinhala, and product suggestions are premature without knowing the daughter's age.
results.what_was_good: The agent correctly identified the need to ask for the daughter's age before finalizing suggestions, and used the user's name naturally.

results.description: Mid-conversation language switch English to Tanglish
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: Aiyo perfect combo la — the Ferrero Rocher Box 24 pcs at Rs. 3,200 is a classic she'll love, and the Enchanted Forest Perfume 50ml at Rs. 4,800 smells amazing machang! Want to grab both together or go with just one?
failures: personalization: name 'Malith' wasn't used in the response — a small missed touch
product_quality: only one product shown per category; showing two options each would give better choice
results.what_was_good: Nailed the Tanglish register instantly with 'Aiyo' and 'machang la', kept it warm and concise, addressed both chocolates and perfumes directly, and ended with a clear next-step question.

results.description: User changes mind after adding to cart
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: No worries, Dilhara! The red roses have been removed. From what's available, the White Lily Bouquet (Rs. 2,600) is a classic, or if you want something a bit fuller, the Mixed Lily Vase (Rs. 3,800) is gorgeous — which one catches your eye?
results.what_was_good: Handled the cart change gracefully with zero friction, immediately pivoted to two concrete lily options with prices, and ended with a natural engaging question to keep the momentum going.

results.description: Product category Kapruka likely doesn't carry — custom furniture
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: Custom-built furniture isn't something we carry, Renuka, but the Storage Ottoman Coffee Table at Rs. 8,900 is a beautiful, functional piece that could anchor your living room nicely. Would you like something more decorative to go with it, or is a statement furniture piece what you're after?
results.what_was_good: The response is concise, honest without over-apologising, pivots naturally to a real product with a price, uses her name, and ends with an engaging follow-up question that keeps the conversation going.

results.description: User gives conflicting information — says gift for friend, then for self
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: The Smart Watch Fitness Black at Rs. 9,900 is a solid pick, Gayan — great for everyday use and tracking your activity! If you want something more classic, the Casio G-Shock at Rs. 15,000 is a timeless choice. Which style do you lean toward?
failures: personalization: Used name well but didn't explicitly leverage the 27-year-old male profile to justify product choices
product_quality: Only two watches shown; more variety or price points would better serve a personal purchase decision
completeness: Could have shown a third option or a price range to help narrow down preferences faster
results.what_was_good: The pivot from gifting to personal shopping was seamless with no awkward callback to the 'friend' context, and the tone was natural and engaging with a clear follow-up question.

results.description: Specific request with no budget mentioned — perfume
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: The Engage Floral Perfume at Rs. 1,900 is a lovely everyday option, Nadeesha, or if you want something a bit more luxe, the Body Shop White Musk at Rs. 3,800 has such a beautiful soft scent! Want to go for something more bold and premium, or keep it light and fresh?
failures: product_quality: Only two products shown — expected qualities call for a range of price points from affordable to premium, with more options tailored to a 25-year-old female (floral, fresh, feminine); two products is too narrow
completeness: Ends with a question before adequately showcasing the full range — the user hasn't seen enough to make a decision, so the conversation isn't sufficiently moved forward
results.what_was_good: Warm, natural tone with good use of the user's name and a conversational follow-up question that feels genuinely helpful rather than robotic.
